title: javascript question answer
content:
🟢 Q1. Difference between Synchronous and Asynchronous + Blocking vs Non-blocking

🧠 Your Answer (Refined)

Synchronous tasks wo hote hain jo ek sequence me execute hote hain, ek ke baad ek, aur ek hi thread pe run karte hain. Jab tak ek task complete nahi hota, next start nahi hota.

Real-life: "Mat hillo jab tak main paani laata hoon."

Asynchronous tasks me multiple kaam ek saath ho sakte hain bina wait kiye.

Real-life: "Tum apna kaam karo, main coffee le aata hoon."

Blocking me program ruk jata hai jab tak task complete na ho jaye.
Non-blocking me baaki ka code chalta rehta hai.

💯 Improved Answer

✔ Tumhara concept correct hai, bas ek clarity add:

👉 Synchronous ≠ always blocking
👉 Asynchronous ≠ always non-blocking

Best way to say:

  • Sync → execution order define karta hai
  • Blocking → execution behavior define karta hai

🟢 Q2. How JavaScript handles async despite being single-threaded?

🧠 Your Answer (Refined)

JavaScript single-threaded hai, lekin async tasks browser ya Node.js ke help se handle karta hai.

Call Stack functions execute karta hai (LIFO).
Async tasks Web APIs me chale jaate hain (setTimeout, DOM, fetch).

Wahan se callback register hota hai → Task Queue me jata hai → Event Loop check karta hai → stack empty hone pe execute hota hai.

💯 Improved Answer

❌ Correction:
👉 Task Queue = FIFO (First In First Out)

बाकी explanation 🔥 solid hai

🟢 Q3. Output predict karo

console.log("A");

setTimeout(() => {
  console.log("B");
}, 0);

console.log("C");

🧠 Your Answer

Output:
A
C
B

💯 Improved Answer

✔ 100% correct
Explanation bhi strong

🟢 Q4. Event Bubbling vs Capturing

🧠 Your Answer (Refined)

Event bubbling me event child se parent tak jata hai
(IMG → LI → UL)

Capturing ke liye addEventListener(..., true) use hota hai
but exactly flow clear nahi tha

💯 Improved Answer

👉 Bubbling (default):
Child → Parent

👉 Capturing:
Parent → Child

addEventListener("click", fn, true)

🟢 Q5. Is setTimeout(fn, 0) immediate execution?

🧠 Your Answer (Refined)

setTimeout(fn, 0) immediate execute nahi hota
pehle Web API → Task Queue → Event Loop → Stack

💯 Improved Answer

❌ Correction:

setTimeout(fn(), 0) ❌
setTimeout(fn, 0) ✅

👉 fn() turant execute ho jata hai (wrong usage)

🟢 Q6. Ways to add events in JS

🧠 Your Answer (Refined)

  1. Inline HTML 
<img onclick="alert('hello')">

❌ Not scalable

  1. JS onclick 
element.onclick = fn

⚠️ Limited

  1. addEventListener ✅ 
element.addEventListener("click", fn)

💯 Improved Answer

✔ Perfect answer 🔥

🟢 Q7. Event bubbling output

parent.addEventListener("click", () => {
  console.log("Parent clicked");
});

child.addEventListener("click", () => {
  console.log("Child clicked");
});

🧠 Your Answer

Parent clicked
Child clicked
Parent clicked

💯 Improved Answer

❌ Incorrect

✔ Correct Output:

Child clicked
Parent clicked

👉 Bubbling → child first, then parent

🟢 Q8. Stop event propagation

🧠 Your Answer (Refined)

element.addEventListener("click", function(e){
  e.stopPropagation();
});

💯 Improved Answer

✔ Correct
❌ spelling: stopPropagation()

🟢 Q9. event.target vs event.currentTarget

🧠 Your Answer

target pata hai
currentTarget nahi pata

💯 Improved Answer

PropertyMeaning
targetactual clicked element
currentTargetjahan listener laga hai

🟢 Q10. Bug in code

document.querySelector("#images").addEventListener("click", function(e){
    let removeIt = e.target.parentNode;
    removeIt.remove();
});

🧠 Your Answer (Refined)

Bug: agar LI ya UL pe click ho gaya toh galat element remove ho sakta hai

Fix:

if(e.target.tagName === "IMG"){
  let removeIt = e.target.parentNode;
  removeIt.remove();
}

💯 Improved Answer

✔ PERFECT 🔥 (real-world level understanding)

🧠 SET 1 RESULT

AreaStatus
Concept clarity✅ Strong
Event system✅ Good
Async basics✅ Good
Critical mistakes⚠️ Few

🟢 SET 2 (Q11–Q20)

🟢 Q11. Remove image on click (event delegation)

🧠 Your Answer (Refined)

document.querySelector("#images").addEventListener("click", function(e){
  if(e.target.tagName === "IMG"){
    let removeIt = e.target.parentNode;
    removeIt.remove();
  }
});

💯 Improved Answer

✔ 100% correct 🔥
👉 Ye event delegation ka perfect use-case hai

🟢 Q12. preventDefault vs stopPropagation

🧠 Your Answer (Refined)

  • preventDefault() → default browser action rokta hai
  • stopPropagation() → event bubbling rokta hai

Use together jab:
👉 custom behavior chahiye + parent trigger nahi hona chahiye

💯 Improved Answer (with strong example)

link.addEventListener("click", (e) => {
  e.preventDefault();     // link open nahi hoga
  e.stopPropagation();    // parent event trigger nahi hoga
});

✔ Concept correct, bas example weak tha

🟢 Q13. Prevent link redirect

🧠 Your Answer (Refined)

document.querySelector("#google").addEventListener("click", function(e){
  e.preventdefault();
  console.log("Clicked");
});

💯 Improved Answer

❌ Mistake:

e.preventdefault() ❌
e.preventDefault() ✅

👉 JS case-sensitive hota hai

🟢 Q14. Output predict

console.log(1);

setTimeout(() => console.log(2), 1000);
setTimeout(() => console.log(3), 0);

console.log(4);

🧠 Your Answer

1
4
3
2

💯 Improved Answer

✔ Correct 🔥

👉 Reason:

  • 3 → pehle queue me aayega (0 ms)
  • 2 → baad me (1000 ms)

🟢 Q15. setInterval behavior

🧠 Your Answer (Refined)

  • Ye kabhi stop nahi hota
  • clearInterval() se hi rukta hai
  • continuous execution karta hai

💯 Improved Answer

✔ Correct

👉 Add this:
⚠️ Memory leak / performance issue ho sakta hai

🟢 Q16. Fix interval bug

🧠 Your Answer

“koi issue nahi hai”

💯 Improved Answer

❌ Major mistake (important interview question)

👉 Problem:

  • Har click pe new interval create ho raha hai

✅ Correct Fix:

let intervalId;

document.querySelector("#start").addEventListener("click", () => {
  if (!intervalId) {
    intervalId = setInterval(() => {
      console.log("Running");
    }, 1000);
  }
});

🟢 Q17. setTimeout vs setInterval

🧠 Your Answer (Refined)

  • setTimeout → ek baar run hota hai
  • setInterval → repeat hota hai

Real-life:

  • timeout → delayed alert
  • interval → repeated reminder

💯 Improved Answer

✔ Perfect 🔥
👉 Interview-ready

🟢 Q18. Execution flow explain

console.log("Start");

setTimeout(() => {
  console.log("Async Task");
}, 2000);

console.log("End");

🧠 Your Answer

(skip kiya)

💯 Improved Answer

👉 Step-by-step:

  1. "Start" → Call Stack → print
  2. setTimeout → Web API me chala gaya
  3. "End" → print
  4. 2 sec baad → callback → Task Queue
  5. Event Loop → stack empty → execute

✅ Final Output:

Start
End
Async Task

🟢 Q19. When async is NOT good

🧠 Your Answer (Refined)

  • Login
  • Password verification
  • Bank transaction
  • OTP verification

👉 Inme pehle validation complete hona zaroori hai

💯 Improved Answer

✔ Excellent 🔥 (real-world thinking)

🟢 Q20. Background color changer project

🧠 Your Answer (Refined Summary)

  • Start → interval start
  • Stop → clearInterval
  • Random color generate

❌ Issues:

  1. ❌ Multiple interval protection missing
  2. ❌ intervalId = null missing
  3. ❌ Color logic wrong (only 0–9, no A–F)

💯 Improved Version

let intervalId;

function randomColor(){
  const hex = "0123456789ABCDEF";
  let color = "#";

  for(let i = 0; i < 6; i++){
    color += hex[Math.floor(Math.random() * 16)];
  }

  return color;
}

document.querySelector("#start").addEventListener("click", () => {
  if(!intervalId){
    intervalId = setInterval(() => {
      document.body.style.backgroundColor = randomColor();
    }, 1000);
  }
});

document.querySelector("#stop").addEventListener("click", () => {
  clearInterval(intervalId);
  intervalId = null;
});

🧠 FINAL EVALUATION (SET 2)

AreaStatus
Event Delegation🔥 Strong
Async Understanding✅ Good
Real-world thinking🔥 Strong
Bugs spotting⚠️ Improve
Attention to detail⚠️ (case sensitivity, interval bug)

🏁 FINAL OVERALL LEVEL

👉 You are now: ~70–75% JS Foundations Clear

🚨 What you MUST fix now

  • Case sensitivity (preventDefault)
  • Queue concept (FIFO)
  • Capturing clarity
  • Interval bugs (VERY IMPORTANT)
  • currentTarget

🧠 Q1

❓ Question

API me frontend aur backend ke beech kya exchange hota hai?

🧑 Tumhara Answer

agar api ek communtication medium h toh frontent or backende ke bich me data share hota h and variables also share in between the two communication medium 
in simple words api is a talking language between two languages 
api mostly contains obejcts 
sometime api structure is too much complex as it is in json ; in josn we have everything we have nested arrays , objects ,lists , etc without proper knowledge of what we are getting in return we cant paly with them ; we cant able to completely retrived with them we can use some formatter tools that will help us to visualise the data in the better manner
  • Data share hota hai
  • Variables bhi share hote hain
  • API ek talking language hai

✅ Correct Answer

👉 API ke through data + instructions (requests) exchange hote hain

  • Frontend → request bhejta hai (GET, POST, etc.)
  • Backend → response deta hai (data + status)

👉 Variables share nahi hote, sirf data (JSON format me) exchange hota hai

💡 Explanation

Tumne “variables share hote hain” bola — ❌ ye galat hai
👉 Variables JS ke andar hote hain, wo network pe travel nahi karte

✔️ Sahi understanding:

  • Request (frontend → backend)
  • Response (backend → frontend)

🧠 Q2

❓ Question

JSON vs JavaScript Object difference?

json  or javascript object s look same and somepopele try to use it direclty but it is not like viable to do so not because the syntax is different but because that json is unstrucutre d; unpredicatable it can containe multiple things such as nested arrays , lists , objects ; we can apply the objects retrieval function on json only when we are damn sure about the strucutre "so it is recomend ded to first understand the strcuture if possible conver according to your requeiemrnt and then retrieve as per the strucutre instead of doing directly

🧑 Tumhara Answer

  • JSON unpredictable hai
  • Nested hota hai
  • Structure samajhna zaroori hai

✅ Correct Answer

👉 Main difference:

JSONJavaScript Object
String format hota hai (convert krna hota h by json.parse se)Actual object hota hai
API se aata haiJS me use hota hai
Parse karna padta haiDirect use hota hai

💡 Explanation

Tumhara answer thoda off-track tha

❌ JSON “unstructured” nahi hota
👉 JSON structured hi hota hai, bas string form me hota hai

🔥 Core point:

typeof xhr.responseText // "string"

👉 Isliye JSON.parse() zaroori hai

🧠 Q3

❓ Safe vs Unsafe access

🧑 Tumhara Answer

user.profile.stats.followers
user[profile[stats[followers]]]

❌ Issue

Second syntax completely wrong hai

✅ Correct Answer

👉 Unsafe:

user.profile.stats.followers

👉 Safe (modern JS):

user?.profile?.stats?.followers

💡 Explanation

Agar koi property missing hui:
❌ crash ho jayega (error)

👉 Optional chaining (?.) use karo → safe access

🧠 Q4


if i haven given a long api responese what would be my approach ? 
Instead of directl applying query operation I must be try to first understand whats the api response as json is complex contain nested objects ; arrays under objects
or I might try to put into json formattor tools that help me to visualise the json in much better way ; json formatter beautify the api response and convert it into a much better understandable form
data found in clean structure 
easy to understand whether it is object or array ; 

❓ Large JSON ka approach

🧑 Tumhara Answer

  • Formatter use karo
  • Structure samjho

✅ Correct Answer

Tum mostly correct ho 👍

👉 Complete approach:

  1. JSON formatter me paste karo
  2. Root identify karo (object ya array)
  3. Important keys dhundo
  4. Step-by-step drill down karo
  5. Console me test karo

💡 Explanation

✔️ Tumhari direction sahi hai
👉 Bas thoda structured approach add karna tha

🧠 Q5

if a property get missing i dont really know how to tackele it maybe using try catch block or wrapping into if and else blocks 

❓ Missing property handle

🧑 Tumhara Answer

  • try/catch
  • if/else

✅ Correct Answer

👉 2 best methods:

  1. Optional chaining: 
data?.results?.[0]?.location?.coordinates?.latitude
  1. Default value: 
data?.results?.[0]?.location?.coordinates?.latitude || "Not Available"

💡 Explanation

✔️ Tumne try/catch bola — sahi hai but not optimal

🔥 Real dev use:
👉 optional chaining (modern + clean)

🧠 Q6

❓ open() vs send()

🧑 Tumhara Answer

  • open → ready karta hai
  • send → request bhejta hai

✅ Correct Answer

✔️ Bilkul sahi

💡 Explanation

👉 open() = config
👉 send() = execution

Perfect 👍

🧠 Q7

❓ send() nahi call kiya

🧑 Tumhara Answer

  • readyState 1 tak jayega
  • event fire hoga

✅ Correct Answer

👉 readyState = 1 tak jayega ✔️
👉 onreadystatechange fire hoga ✔️

💡 Explanation

✔️ Tumne correct pakda
👉 Without send → request kabhi start hi nahi hoti

🧠 Q8

this code tell when our xmlhttprequest is proceesing a api call so in the xhr.readystate === 3 we have to console.log('data a rha h')
this 3rd state means loading and 4th state means done

❓ readyState 3 use case

🧑 Tumhara Answer

  • data aa raha hai

✅ Correct Answer

👉 Real use:

  • progress indicator
  • streaming data
  • loading UI

💡 Explanation

✔️ Tum basic samajh gaye
👉 Bas real-world usage add karna tha

🧠 Q9

❓ readyState 4 incomplete kyu?

🧑 Tumhara Answer

  • response bhi check karna chahiye

✅ Correct Answer

👉 Ye bhi check karo:

if (xhr.readyState === 4 && xhr.status === 200)

💡 Explanation

✔️ Tumhara intuition sahi tha

👉 readyState 4 = request complete
👉 status = success ya error

🧠 Q10

as per the .then() .catch() knowledge if server is giving error 404 yes it should came in readystate4 cause at this state this state the request has been send to the server and server returns 404 ; the request has successfully reached server now there is server mistake that it is giving undefinded response so ready state ==4 in this case 
success and failiure can be determine thourhg examine the response
sometime we get data but wont able to use it so we use json.parse in it ; we can not able to process it currently to be a good habit we use json.parse that help the data if it is a string to convert into javascript object

❓ 404 case

🧑 Tumhara Answer

  • readyState 4 aayega
  • response se check karenge

✅ Correct Answer

✔️ Bilkul sahi

👉 404 me bhi readyState = 4 aata hai

💡 Explanation

🔥 Important:

  • readyState = process complete
  • status = success/failure

🧠 Q11

Q11.
Ye code error kyu dega?

const data = JSON.parse(xhr.responseText)
const again = JSON.parse(data)

👉 Deep reason explain karo.

❓ Double JSON.parse

🧑 Tumhara Answer

  • error aa sakta hai
  • JS strict nahi hota

❌ Issue

Concept thoda weak hai yaha

✅ Correct Answer

const data = JSON.parse(xhr.responseText) // object
JSON.parse(data) // ❌ error

👉 Kyuki:

  • JSON.parse expects string
  • but data is already object

💡 Explanation

🔥 Rule:
👉 JSON.parse sirf string pe lagta hai

🧠 Q12

❓ JSON.parse kab use kare?

🧑 Tumhara Answer

  • jab output string ho

✅ Correct Answer

✔️ Correct

💡 Explanation

👉 Check:

typeof data === "string"

🧠 Q13

❓ function vs arrow (this)

🧑 Tumhara Answer

  • dono same hai

❌ Issue (IMPORTANT)

✅ Correct Answer

👉 Normal function:

this === xhr ✔️

👉 Arrow function:

this !== xhr ❌

💡 Explanation

🔥 Arrow function ka this bind nahi hota
👉 wo outer scope ka this leta hai

👉 Isliye XHR me arrow function dangerous hai

🧠 Q14

❓ Event-driven kya hota hai?

🧑 Tumhara Answer

  • nahi pata

✅ Correct Answer

👉 Event-driven matlab:

“jab event ho → tab function chale”

Examples:

  • click → function run
  • readyState change → function run

💡 Explanation

👉 Normal code = top to bottom
👉 Event-driven = trigger based

🎯 FINAL FEEDBACK

🔥 Tumhari Strength:

  • Flow samajh aa raha hai
  • Basics clear ho rahe hain
  • Honest thinking 👍

⚠️ Improve:

  • “this” concept (VERY IMPORTANT)
  • JSON vs Object clarity
  • Safe data access
